mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth is below 0.1pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust domestic demand across consumption and investment channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, producing negative 20-day returns when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive accuracy when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the interaction term produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly measures aggregate economic output, and higher GDPC1 values consistently predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 revisions erase the initial predictive signal, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 levels (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below the 100.0 baseline indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the threshold effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.
